contradictions.claim: 50% reduction in carbon emissions across supply chain by 2030
contradictions.evidence_text: Supply chain emissions (Scope 3) increased 6% in FY2023. Current trajectory does not align with 2030 targets without significant supplier transitions not yet underway.

contradictions.claim: Nike Manufacturing Index ensures supplier accountability
contradictions.evidence_text: At least 12 Nike suppliers cited for labor violations in Vietnam and Cambodia in 2022-2023, including excessive overtime, wage theft, and union suppression. Nike Manufacturing Index scores not publicly disclosed.

contradictions.claim: Worker wellbeing programs across South Asian supply chain
contradictions.evidence_text: Nike's Indian and Pakistani suppliers documented for 12-16 hour shifts, below-minimum wages, and no grievance mechanism. Nike's published wellbeing claims do not address these factories.

contradictions.claim: 100% renewable energy in owned and operated facilities
contradictions.evidence_text: Owned and operated facilities represent less than 5% of total energy footprint. Scope 3 emissions (supply chain, 95% of footprint) remain predominantly fossil fuel-powered.

contradictions.claim: Nachhaltige Materialien gemäß EU Green Claims Directive
contradictions.evidence_source: EU Green Claims Directive Enforcement, Netherlands 2023
contradictions.evidence_text: Nike received formal inquiry from Dutch Consumer Authority regarding 'sustainable materials' claims that could not be substantiated under forthcoming EU Green Claims standards.

sec_filing.public_claim: 50% reduction in carbon emissions across supply chain by 2030
sec_filing.sec_language: We have set targets to reduce Scope 3 emissions. Achieving these targets depends substantially on the actions of third-party suppliers over which we have limited control. There can be no assurance these targets will be achieved.
sec_filing.delta_description: Public claims state a specific 50% supply chain reduction commitment; 10-K filing discloses no assurance of achievement and limited supplier control — the two disclosures are materially inconsistent.

news_violations.headline: Nike suppliers in Vietnam cited for widespread labor violations
news_violations.summary: Documented violations at 12+ Nike suppliers including illegal overtime, union suppression, and physical abuse reported by workers

news_violations.headline: Norway Consumer Authority finds Nike sustainability claims misleading
news_violations.summary: Forbrukertilsynet found Nike's sustainability marketing claims lacked substantiation required under Norwegian marketing law

news_violations.headline: Nike faces greenwashing lawsuit over recycled materials claims
news_violations.summary: Class action alleges Nike misrepresented percentage of recycled content in key product lines

sample_reviews: The sustainability story we tell externally doesn't match what happens with our factory partners. It's mostly marketing.
Supply chain issues get buried. Anyone who raises them is told to stay in their lane.
Genuinely proud of some programs but the supply chain side is a black box even internally.
